name: Baked Turnover
recipeIngredient: 1 Large Egg White, beaten with a little water for glazing
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour and cold butter in a food processor or cut the butter into coarse crumbs using a pastry cutter. Add the egg yolks, salt, and sour cream. Process or knead lightly until the dough forms a ball.
2. Cut the dough in half and wrap each piece in plastic wrap. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
4. Roll out one half of the dough on a lightly floured work surface to about 1/4-inch thickness. Cut into rounds using a 3-inch cutter or wine glass.
5. Place a spoonful of filling on one side of each round, fold the top over, and brush with a little egg glaze. Seal with a fork or pastry cutter for a fancy edge.
6. Place each turnover on a lightly greased or non-stick baking sheet, brushing with the glaze, then sprinkle with sugar crystals.
7. Bake in the preheated oven for about 20 minutes. Cool on wire racks.
NOTE:
This is a short dough, so use a non-stick baking sheet if available. If not, brush as little grease on the sheet as possible using a pastry brush or new paintbrush.
